-- Hoogle documentation, generated by Haddock
-- See Hoogle, http://www.haskell.org/hoogle/


-- | Matchable Functors
--   
--   Matchable Functors
@package matchable
@version 0.1.0

module Data.Matchable.Orphans

module Data.Matchable

-- | Containers that allows exact structural matching of two containers.
class (Eq1 t, Functor t) => Matchable t

-- | Decides if two structures match exactly. If they match, return zipped
--   version of them.
--   
--   <pre>
--   zipMatch ta tb = Just tab
--   </pre>
--   
--   holds if and only if both of
--   
--   <pre>
--   ta = fmap fst tab
--   tb = fmap snd tab
--   </pre>
--   
--   holds. Otherwise, <tt>zipMatch ta tb = Nothing</tt>.
--   
--   For example, the type signature of <tt>zipMatch</tt> on the list
--   Functor <tt>[]</tt> reads as follows:
--   
--   <pre>
--   zipMatch :: [a] -&gt; [b] -&gt; Maybe [(a,b)]
--   </pre>
--   
--   <tt>zipMatch as bs</tt> returns <tt>Just (zip as bs)</tt> if the
--   lengths of two given lists are same, and returns <tt>Nothing</tt>
--   otherwise.
--   
--   <h4>Example</h4>
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; zipMatch [1, 2, 3] ['a', 'b', 'c']
--   Just [(1,'a'),(2,'b'),(3,'c')]
--   
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; zipMatch [1, 2, 3] ['a', 'b']
--   Nothing
--   </pre>
zipMatch :: Matchable t => t a -> t b -> Maybe (t (a, b))

-- | Match two structures. If they match, zip them with given function
--   <tt>(a -&gt; b -&gt; Maybe c)</tt>. Passed function can make whole
--   match fail by returning <tt>Nothing</tt>.
--   
--   A definition of <a>zipMatchWith</a> must satisfy:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li>If there is a pair <tt>(tab, tc)</tt> such that fulfills all
--   following three conditions, then <tt>zipMatchWith f ta tb = Just
--   tc</tt>.<ol><li><pre>ta = fmap fst tab</pre></li><li><pre>tb = fmap
--   snd tab</pre></li><li><pre>fmap (uncurry f) tab = fmap Just
--   tc</pre></li></ol></li>
--   <li>If there are no such pair, <tt>zipMatchWith f ta tb =
--   Nothing</tt>.</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   If <tt>t</tt> is also <a>Traversable</a>, the last condition can be
--   dropped and the equation can be stated without using <tt>tc</tt>.
--   
--   <pre>
--   zipMatchWith f ta tb = traverse (uncurry f) tab
--   </pre>
--   
--   <tt>zipMatch</tt> can be defined in terms of <tt>zipMatchWith</tt>.
--   And if <tt>t</tt> is also <tt>Traversable</tt>, <tt>zipMatchWith</tt>
--   can be defined in terms of <tt>zipMatch</tt>. When you implement both
--   of them by hand, keep their relation in the way the default
--   implementation is.
--   
--   <pre>
--   zipMatch             = zipMatchWith (curry pure)
--   zipMatchWith f ta tb = zipMatch ta tb &gt;&gt;= traverse (uncurry f)
--   </pre>
zipMatchWith :: Matchable t => (a -> b -> Maybe c) -> t a -> t b -> Maybe (t c)

-- | <pre>
--   zipzipMatch = zipMatchWith zipMatch
--   </pre>
zipzipMatch :: (Matchable t, Matchable u) => t (u a) -> t (u b) -> Maybe (t (u (a, b)))

-- | <tt>Matchable t</tt> implies <tt>Functor t</tt>. It is not recommended
--   to implement <tt>fmap</tt> through this function, so it is named
--   <tt>fmapRecovered</tt> but not <tt>fmapDefault</tt>.
fmapRecovered :: (Matchable t) => (a -> b) -> t a -> t b

-- | <tt>Matchable t</tt> implies <tt>Eq a =&gt; Eq (t a)</tt>.
eqDefault :: (Matchable t, Eq a) => t a -> t a -> Bool

-- | <tt>Matchable t</tt> implies <tt>Eq1 t</tt>.
liftEqDefault :: (Matchable t) => (a -> b -> Bool) -> t a -> t b -> Bool

-- | An instance of Matchable can be implemened through GHC Generics. You
--   only need to do two things: Make your type <tt>Functor</tt> and
--   <tt>Generic1</tt>.
--   
--   <h4>Example</h4>
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; :set -XDeriveFunctor
--   
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; :set -XDeriveGeneric
--   
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; :{
--     data MyTree label a = Leaf a | Node label [MyTree label a]
--       deriving (Show, Read, Eq, Ord, Functor, Generic1)
--   :}
--   </pre>
--   
--   Then you can use <tt>genericZipMatchWith</tt> to implement
--   <tt>zipMatchWith</tt> method. You also need <tt>Eq1</tt> instance, but
--   <a>liftEqDefault</a> is provided.
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; :{
--     instance (Eq label) =&gt; Matchable (MyTree label) where
--       zipMatchWith = genericZipMatchWith
--     instance (Eq label) =&gt; Eq1 (MyTree label) where
--       liftEq = liftEqDefault
--     :}
--   </pre>
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; zipMatch (Node "foo" [Leaf 1, Leaf 2]) (Node "foo" [Leaf 'a', Leaf 'b'])
--   Just (Node "foo" [Leaf (1,'a'),Leaf (2,'b')])
--   
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; zipMatch (Node "foo" [Leaf 1, Leaf 2]) (Node "bar" [Leaf 'a', Leaf 'b'])
--   Nothing
--   
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; zipMatch (Node "foo" [Leaf 1]) (Node "foo" [])
--   Nothing
--   </pre>
class Matchable' t

-- | zipMatchWith via Generics.
genericZipMatchWith :: (Generic1 t, Matchable' (Rep1 t)) => (a -> b -> Maybe c) -> t a -> t b -> Maybe (t c)
instance Data.Matchable.Matchable' GHC.Generics.V1
instance Data.Matchable.Matchable' GHC.Generics.U1
instance Data.Matchable.Matchable' GHC.Generics.Par1
instance Data.Matchable.Matchable f => Data.Matchable.Matchable' (GHC.Generics.Rec1 f)
instance GHC.Classes.Eq c => Data.Matchable.Matchable' (GHC.Generics.K1 i c)
instance Data.Matchable.Matchable' f => Data.Matchable.Matchable' (GHC.Generics.M1 i c f)
instance (Data.Matchable.Matchable' f, Data.Matchable.Matchable' g) => Data.Matchable.Matchable' (f GHC.Generics.:+: g)
instance (Data.Matchable.Matchable' f, Data.Matchable.Matchable' g) => Data.Matchable.Matchable' (f GHC.Generics.:*: g)
instance (Data.Matchable.Matchable f, Data.Matchable.Matchable' g) => Data.Matchable.Matchable' (f GHC.Generics.:.: g)
instance Data.Matchable.Matchable Data.Functor.Identity.Identity
instance GHC.Classes.Eq c => Data.Matchable.Matchable (Data.Functor.Const.Const c)
instance Data.Matchable.Matchable GHC.Base.Maybe
instance Data.Matchable.Matchable []
instance Data.Matchable.Matchable Data.List.NonEmpty.NonEmpty
instance GHC.Classes.Eq e => Data.Matchable.Matchable ((,) e)
instance GHC.Classes.Eq e => Data.Matchable.Matchable (Data.Either.Either e)
instance GHC.Classes.Eq k => Data.Matchable.Matchable (Data.Map.Internal.Map k)
instance (Data.Matchable.Matchable f, Data.Matchable.Matchable g) => Data.Matchable.Matchable (Data.Functor.Compose.Compose f g)
instance Data.Matchable.Matchable f => Data.Matchable.Matchable (Control.Monad.Free.Free f)
instance Data.Matchable.Matchable f => Data.Matchable.Matchable (Control.Comonad.Cofree.Cofree f)

module Data.Matchable.Equate
data Equate f a b
(:<=>) :: a -> b -> Equate f a b
(:==>) :: a -> f b -> Equate f a b
(:<==) :: f a -> b -> Equate f a b
swapDirection :: Equate f a b -> Equate f b a
equate :: (Foldable f, Matchable f) => Free f a -> Free f b -> Maybe [Equate (Free f) a b]
instance Data.Traversable.Traversable f => Data.Traversable.Traversable (Data.Matchable.Equate.Equate f a)
instance Data.Foldable.Foldable f => Data.Foldable.Foldable (Data.Matchable.Equate.Equate f a)
instance GHC.Base.Functor f => GHC.Base.Functor (Data.Matchable.Equate.Equate f a)
instance (GHC.Classes.Ord (f a), GHC.Classes.Ord (f b), GHC.Classes.Ord b, GHC.Classes.Ord a) => GHC.Classes.Ord (Data.Matchable.Equate.Equate f a b)
instance (GHC.Classes.Eq (f a), GHC.Classes.Eq (f b), GHC.Classes.Eq b, GHC.Classes.Eq a) => GHC.Classes.Eq (Data.Matchable.Equate.Equate f a b)
instance (GHC.Read.Read (f a), GHC.Read.Read (f b), GHC.Read.Read b, GHC.Read.Read a) => GHC.Read.Read (Data.Matchable.Equate.Equate f a b)
instance (GHC.Show.Show (f a), GHC.Show.Show (f b), GHC.Show.Show b, GHC.Show.Show a) => GHC.Show.Show (Data.Matchable.Equate.Equate f a b)
instance GHC.Base.Functor f => Data.Bifunctor.Bifunctor (Data.Matchable.Equate.Equate f)
instance Data.Foldable.Foldable f => Data.Bifoldable.Bifoldable (Data.Matchable.Equate.Equate f)
instance Data.Traversable.Traversable f => Data.Bitraversable.Bitraversable (Data.Matchable.Equate.Equate f)

module Data.Matchable.PatternMatch
type Pattern t a = Free (Base t) a
match :: (Recursive t, Foldable (Base t), Matchable (Base t)) => Pattern t a -> t -> Maybe [(a, t)]
